title: PermissionsConfig::default_allow Vec<String> globs are loaded but never validated
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
security
## Location
src/config/permissions.rs:7-17
## Violation
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Deserialize, Clone, Default)]
pub struct PermissionsConfig {
    #[serde(default)]
    pub enabled: bool,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub default_allow: Vec<String>,
}
```

The `default_allow` field accepts arbitrary glob strings from YAML with no validation: a typo (`/etc/**` instead of `./etc/**`) silently pre-allows access to system paths. An unbounded glob like `**` or `*` permits ALL operations matching the tool's primary argument — effectively disabling the permission system while LOOKING enabled. A regex-style entry like `.*` is treated as a literal glob (probably matching nothing) so a user copying a regex pattern from another tool gets silent failure.

There is no per-tool scoping in this type: a glob applies to whatever tool is running, with no way to say "this path is allowed for Read but not Write". The current PermissionManager docstring (acp.rs:135) suggests "matched against the tool's primary argument" — but the config type does not enforce or document that contract.
## Mandated refactor
Make `default_allow` a `Vec<AllowEntry>` where `AllowEntry { tool: Option<String>, pattern: String, mode: AllowMode }`, with `Deserialize` validating that `pattern` is a parseable glob via the `globset` crate. Reject unbounded patterns (`**`, `*`) at parse time unless the config also includes `permissions.allow_unbounded_globs = true`. Emit a warning at load if `default_allow` is non-empty but `enabled = false` — almost certainly a misconfiguration.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
